observations: Well-implemented Lloyd-Max quantizer with proper convergence checking
Orthogonal rotation preserves vector norms correctly
QJL projection uses correct scaling for inner product preservation
TurboQuantKeyCompressor and KVCacheQuantizer provide clean APIs
Group quantization for values with configurable group sizes
Comprehensive tests for Lloyd-Max quantization convergence and accuracy
Orthogonal rotation tests verify norm preservation and reversibility
QJL projection tests verify inner product preservation within expected bounds
Key compression tests achieve >0.99 cosine similarity with 3-bit quantization
Value compression tests achieve >0.94 cosine similarity with 2-bit quantization
Modular design with separate classes for each quantization component
TurboQuantKeyCompressor combines rotation, QJL projection, and quantization
KVCacheQuantizer provides high-level API for compressing full KV cache
Proper use of PyTorch for GPU acceleration

nonBlocking.description: QJL projection inner product preservation test has high variance - average error threshold is 100%
nonBlocking.suggestion: Consider increasing test sample size or using statistical bounds rather than point estimates

sharedStateObservations.observation: Johnson-Lindenstrauss projection for QJL uses scaling factor 1/sqrt(proj_dim) to preserve inner products in expectation
Lloyd-Max quantization converges within 100 iterations with tolerance 1e-6 for typical data distributions
